definitions: To be in a horizontal position on a surface.
To remain in a particular state or position.
To tell an untruth; to intentionally make a false statement.
examples: He likes to lie on the beach.
The city lies on the coast.
The problem lies with the management.
She would never lie to her friends.
pastTenses: lay, have lain (for 'to recline'); lied, have lied (for 'to tell an untruth')
